entries.title: A quiet start
entries.body: Spent the morning journaling and walking. Feeling settled and grateful for the slower pace.

entries.title: Offsite recap
entries.body: Long day at the team offsite. Good conversations with Sam about the roadmap, but I'm wiped out.

entries.title: Dinner with Mom
entries.body: Family dinner at home. Mom made her usual and Alex stopped by. Felt warm and easy.

entries.title: Rough Monday
entries.body: Hard to focus today. Too many context switches at work. Trying to be kind to myself about it.

entries.title: Coffee with Alex
entries.body: Caught up with Alex over coffee. We talked about the trip we keep almost planning. Left feeling lighter.

entries.title: Shipped the feature
entries.body: Finally shipped the thing Sam and I have been grinding on. Tired but proud. Took a walk to celebrate.

entries.title: Quiet evening
entries.body: Read on the porch for an hour with no phone. Small thing, but it reset my head.

entries.title: Off day
entries.body: Didn't sleep well and it showed. Low energy all morning. Logging it so I notice the pattern.

entries.title: Lake trail run
entries.body: First proper run in two weeks. Took the lake loop with Dana — legs heavy at the start, great by the end.

entries.title: Berlin trip planning
entries.body: Alex and I finally sat down and turned the maybe-trip into a real one. Flights booked for the 19th. It's happening.

entries.title: 1:1 with Lena
entries.body: Monthly check-in with Lena. Talked through the quarterly review and what I want from the next six months.

entries.title: Tuesday reset
entries.body: Slow morning, tidy desk, short walk before lunch. Trying to make the small resets a habit instead of an emergency measure.

entries.title: Quarterly review prep
entries.body: Spent most of the day building the review deck with Sam. The numbers tell a better story than I feared. Friday should be fine.

entries.title: Berlin, day one
entries.body: Trip log placeholder — landing TXL replacement at 10:40, drop bags at the Kreuzberg flat, then walk until our legs give out.

entries.title: Alex's birthday picnic
entries.body: Planning entry — Theo is flying in, Dana's bringing the cake, I'm on blankets and the playlist. Want to write down what actually happens here afterward.

places.name: Lake trail
places.notes: 5.2 km loop around the reservoir; gravel, mostly shaded.

places.name: Berlin
places.notes: June trip with Alex — staying in Kreuzberg.

places.name: Riverside Park
places.notes: Good picnic lawn near the north entrance.

entry_parts.title: Roadmap discussion
entry_parts.body:
## What we agreed

- Q3 focus stays on **sync reliability**, not new surface area
- Sam owns the migration plan, I own the rollout comms
- Revisit the mobile editor *after* the quarterly review

The debate about scope got heated but landed somewhere sane.

entry_parts.title: Action items
entry_parts.body:
- [ ] Send offsite summary to the team
- [ ] Book follow-up with Sam on the migration plan
- [x] Expense the train tickets

entry_parts.title: Train ride home
entry_parts.body: Watched the coastline go by and didn't open the laptop once. Best decision of the day.

entry_parts.title: The evening
entry_parts.body:
Mom made the lemon chicken with rosemary from the garden. Alex showed up with a terrible bottle of wine and a great story about his landlord. We stayed at the table until almost eleven.

> "You don't have to earn a slow Sunday." — Mom, mid-dishes

entry_parts.title: Recipe to steal
entry_parts.body: Lemon chicken: marinate 2h in lemon + garlic + rosemary, roast at 200C for 45 min, baste twice. Ask Mom about the pan sauce ratio.

entry_parts.title: What shipped
entry_parts.body:
## Release notes (internal)

- Per-field **last-write-wins** merge on sync conflicts
- Offline queue survives app restarts
- New conflict badge in the record editor

Six weeks of work compressed into three bullet points. Feels about right.

entry_parts.title: Bugs closed this sprint
entry_parts.body: Down from 31 open at the start of the sprint.

entry_parts.title: Retro thought
entry_parts.body: The last 10% took 40% of the time, again. Next time: cut scope at the two-thirds mark instead of pretending the deadline will move.

entry_parts.title: Distance (km)
entry_parts.body: Lake loop, full circuit.

entry_parts.title: Time (min)
entry_parts.body: About 6:00/km — slow, but it's a base to build on.

entry_parts.title: How it felt
entry_parts.body: First 2 km were a negotiation, the rest was easy. Dana set the pace and talked the whole way, which helped. Knee was fine.

entry_parts.title: Itinerary draft
entry_parts.body:
## Berlin — 4 nights

**Fri 19** — land 10:40, Kreuzberg flat, Markthalle Neun for lunch, evening walk along the canal

**Sat 20** — Museum Island in the morning, Mauerpark if the weather holds

**Sun 21** — day trip to Potsdam *or* a lazy Tempelhofer Feld day

**Mon 22** — galleries + record shops, dinner with Alex's cousin

**Tue 23** — fly home 14:20

entry_parts.title: Nights booked
entry_parts.body: Kreuzberg flat, confirmation in email.

entry_parts.title: Budget
entry_parts.body: Flights 380, flat 460, daily ~80 x 5 = 400. Call it 1300 with a buffer. Fine.

entry_parts.title: Packing list
entry_parts.body:
- [ ] Rain shell (Berlin in June, you never know)
- [ ] Camera + spare battery
- [ ] Comfortable shoes, broken in
- [ ] EU plug adapter
- [ ] One nice shirt for the cousin dinner

entry_parts.title: Agenda
entry_parts.body:
1. Quarterly review — what story are we telling?
2. Growth: I want to own a project end-to-end next half
3. The on-call rotation is too thin (again)

entry_parts.title: Decisions
entry_parts.body: Lena agreed I present the sync section at the review myself. On-call: she'll raise headcount with the directors, no promises.

entry_parts.title: Follow-ups
entry_parts.body:
- [ ] Draft the sync section slides by Wednesday
- [ ] Send Lena the incident summary from May
- [ ] Think about what "end-to-end" project I'd actually pitch

entry_parts.title: Three good things
entry_parts.body:
1. Coffee on the steps before anyone else was up
2. Inbox zero by noon (briefly, but still)
3. Dana sent a photo from the trail — we're making the run a weekly thing

entry_parts.title: Sleep (h)
entry_parts.body: Best night in two weeks. The no-phone-after-ten rule seems to be the variable.

entry_parts.title: Deck outline
entry_parts.body:
## Quarterly review — sync section

1. **Where we started** — 31 open sync bugs, weekly conflict complaints
2. **What we shipped** — per-field LWW, offline queue, conflict badge
3. **The numbers** — conflict reports down 70%, support tickets halved
4. **Next quarter** — delta pushes, schema reconciliation

entry_parts.title: Slides drafted
entry_parts.body: Out of a planned 11. The two missing ones are the hard ones, obviously.

entry_parts.title: Open questions
entry_parts.body: Do we show the May incident or bury it? Sam says show it — the recovery is the story. He's probably right.

entry_parts.title: Morning
entry_parts.body: Landed early, flat is better than the photos. First döner within the hour, as is law.

entry_parts.title: Canal walk
entry_parts.body: Golden hour along the Landwehrkanal — swans, swing dancers, someone selling records off a blanket.

entry_parts.title: Steps
entry_parts.body: New personal record for a travel day.

entry_parts.title: Dinner notes
entry_parts.body: Tiny Vietnamese place off Oranienstraße, no menu in English, best meal in months. Write down the name tomorrow before I forget — it's the one with the green door.
